text: JINJA DISTRICT BUSINESS REGISTRATION. Jinja is Eastern Uganda's commercial hub and a growing industrial city (recently granted city status). Trading license: Apply at Jinja City Council offices on Main Street. Cost: UGX 50,000-150,000/year depending on business category. Small retail/service businesses (phone repair, salon, food stall): UGX 50,000-80,000/year. Medium businesses (workshop, wholesale, transport): UGX 100,000-150,000/year. Documents needed: National ID, passport photos (2), tenancy agreement or land title, completed application form (available at council offices). Processing time: 2-3 working days. District Commercial Officer (DCO) office: Jinja City Council, Ground Floor, Main Street. The DCO can help with business registration advice, trade fair participation, and linking to government programmes like Emyooga and PDM. Jinja-specific opportunities: tourism businesses (Source of the Nile), agro-processing (sugarcane from Kakira), and fishing enterprises (Lake Victoria access).

MBARARA DISTRICT BUSINESS REGISTRATION. Mbarara is the commercial capital of Western Uganda with a rapidly growing economy driven by dairy, agriculture, and trade with neighbouring DRC and Rwanda. Trading license: Apply at Mbarara City Council revenue office. Cost: UGX 40,000-120,000/year. Micro-enterprises (roadside stalls, boda-boda, small services): UGX 40,000-60,000/year. Small to medium businesses: UGX 80,000-120,000/year. Documents: National ID, 2 passport photos, tenancy agreement, LC1 letter confirming business location. Processing: 1-3 working days. District Commercial Officer: Mbarara City Council offices, High Street. Special considerations: cross-border traders need additional documentation (URSB certificate recommended). Mbarara has active business associations — Mbarara District Chamber of Commerce offers networking and advocacy. Key sectors: dairy farming and processing (Ankole cattle), banana trade, and transit trade to western borders. The Mbarara Milk Collection Centre connects dairy farmers to processors like Pearl Dairy.

GULU DISTRICT BUSINESS REGISTRATION. Gulu is Northern Uganda's largest commercial centre, experiencing post-conflict economic recovery with significant NGO and government investment. Trading license: Apply at Gulu City Council offices. Cost: UGX 30,000-100,000/year — generally lower than Kampala due to lower commercial activity levels. Micro-businesses: UGX 30,000-50,000/year. Small-medium: UGX 60,000-100,000/year. Documents: National ID, passport photos, tenancy agreement or LC1 letter, completed application form. Processing: 1-5 working days (may be slower due to fewer staff). District Commercial Officer: Gulu City Council, Commercial Department. Special notes: Gulu has strong NGO presence — many youth enterprises supply goods and services to NGO operations (catering, transport, cleaning, printing). Northern Uganda Social Action Fund (NUSAF) provides additional funding for youth enterprises in the region. Key sectors: agriculture (sesame, sunflower, shea nuts), transport (connecting to South Sudan border trade), and construction (ongoing post-conflict rebuilding).

FORT PORTAL DISTRICT BUSINESS REGISTRATION. Fort Portal is the tourism capital of Uganda, gateway to Rwenzori Mountains, Kibale National Park, and the crater lakes region in Kabarole district. Trading license: Apply at Fort Portal Tourism City Council offices. Cost: UGX 40,000-120,000/year. Tourism-related businesses (lodges, tour guides, craft shops) may require additional UWA (Uganda Wildlife Authority) coordination. Documents: National ID, passport photos, proof of premises, LC1 recommendation letter. Processing: 2-5 working days. District Commercial Officer: Fort Portal Tourism City Council, Main Office. Special considerations: tourism businesses need to register with Uganda Tourism Board (UTB) — annual fee UGX 100,000-500,000 depending on category. Fort Portal-specific opportunities: eco-tourism (guided hikes, bird watching), tea and coffee farming (Rwenzori region), chocolate production (Semuliki cocoa), and craft markets targeting the 300,000+ annual tourists visiting the region's national parks.

MBALE DISTRICT BUSINESS REGISTRATION. Mbale is the commercial hub of Eastern Uganda's Bugisu sub-region, famous for Arabica coffee production on the slopes of Mount Elgon. Trading license: Apply at Mbale City Council revenue department. Cost: UGX 40,000-100,000/year. Small retail and services: UGX 40,000-60,000/year. Medium enterprises: UGX 70,000-100,000/year. Documents: National ID, 2 passport photos, tenancy agreement or ownership proof, LC1 letter. Processing: 2-3 working days. District Commercial Officer: Mbale City Council, Republic Street offices. Special notes: Mbale Industrial Park (under development) will offer discounted workspace for manufacturing enterprises. Cross-border trade with Kenya (Malaba border, 50km away) creates opportunities for import/export businesses — consider URSB registration for credibility with customs. Key sectors: Arabica coffee processing and export (Bugisu Cooperative Union), wheat farming (highest altitude wheat in Uganda), tourism (Mount Elgon hiking, Sipi Falls), and cross-border trade. Mbale Bugisu Cooperative Union is one of Uganda's oldest and most successful cooperative models.
